   463	    def test_main_import_no_v1(self):
   464	        """Verify main.py imports without v1 code."""
   465	        import sys
   696	    def test_refresh_updates_paths_without_losing_backup(self, setup_store_with_two_packs):
   697	        """Test that refresh after use/back updates paths but preserves backup."""
   698	        store, ui_root = setup_store_with_two_packs
   699	        
   700	        from src.store.ui_attach import UIAttacher
   701	        import yaml
   702	        
   703	        attacher = UIAttacher(
   704	            layout=store.layout,
   705	            ui_roots={"comfyui": ui_root},
   706	        )
   707	        
   708	        yaml_path = ui_root / "extra_model_paths.yaml"
   709	        backup_path = ui_root / "extra_model_paths.yaml.synapse.bak"
   710	        
   711	        # Create original YAML
   712	        original_content = {"user_paths": {"custom": "/my/path"}}
   713	        with open(yaml_path, "w") as f:
   714	            yaml.dump(original_content, f)
   715	        original_bytes = yaml_path.read_bytes()
   716	        
   717	        # First attach - creates backup
   718	        result1 = attacher.attach("comfyui", use_yaml=True)
   719	        assert result1.success
   720	        assert backup_path.exists()
   721	        backup_bytes = backup_path.read_bytes()
   722	        
   723	        # Read synapse paths after first attach
   724	        with open(yaml_path) as f:
   725	            content1 = yaml.safe_load(f)
   726	        synapse_paths1 = content1.get("synapse", {})
   727	        
   728	        # Simulate view change (would happen after use/back)
   729	        # Just update the active view target
   730	        active_path = store.layout.view_active_path("comfyui")
   731	        
   732	        # Refresh attach (simulating what happens after use/back)
   733	        result2 = attacher.attach("comfyui", use_yaml=True)
   734	        assert result2.success
   735	        
   736	        # Backup should still exist and be unchanged
   737	        assert backup_path.exists(), "Backup should still exist after refresh"
   738	        assert backup_path.read_bytes() == backup_bytes, "Backup should not change"
   739	        assert backup_path.read_bytes() == original_bytes, "Backup should match original"
   740	        
   741	        # YAML should still have synapse section and user content
   742	        with open(yaml_path) as f:
   743	            content2 = yaml.safe_load(f)
   744	        
   745	        assert "synapse" in content2
   746	        assert "user_paths" in content2, "User content should be preserved"
   747	    
   748	    def test_refresh_attached_only_updates_attached_uis(self, setup_store_with_two_packs):
   749	        """Test that refresh_attached only updates UIs that are already attached."""
   750	        store, ui_root = setup_store_with_two_packs
   751	        
   752	        from src.store.ui_attach import UIAttacher
   753	        
   754	        # Create second UI root (forge) but don't attach it
   755	        forge_root = ui_root.parent / "Forge"
   756	        forge_root.mkdir(parents=True)
   757	        (forge_root / "models" / "Lora").mkdir(parents=True)
   758	        
   759	        attacher = UIAttacher(
   760	            layout=store.layout,
   761	            ui_roots={"comfyui": ui_root, "forge": forge_root},
   762	        )
   763	        
   764	        # Attach only comfyui
   765	        attacher.attach("comfyui", use_yaml=True)
   766	        
   767	        # Verify comfyui is attached, forge is not
   768	        comfyui_status = attacher.status("comfyui")
   769	        forge_status = attacher.status("forge")
   770	        
   771	        assert comfyui_status["attached"], "ComfyUI should be attached"
   772	        assert not forge_status["attached"], "Forge should NOT be attached"
   773	        
   774	        # Call refresh_attached
   775	        results = attacher.refresh_attached(["comfyui", "forge"])
   776	        
   777	        # Only comfyui should be in results (forge was skipped)
   778	        assert "comfyui" in results, "ComfyUI should be refreshed"
   779	        assert "forge" not in results, "Forge should not be refreshed (was detached)"
   780	        
   781	        # Forge should still be detached
   782	        forge_status_after = attacher.status("forge")
   783	        assert not forge_status_after["attached"], "Forge should still be detached"
   784	    
   785	    def test_full_attach_detach_reattach_cycle(self, setup_store_with_two_packs):
   786	        """Test complete attach -> detach -> reattach cycle preserves behavior."""
   787	        store, ui_root = setup_store_with_two_packs
   788	        
   789	        from src.store.ui_attach import UIAttacher
   790	        import yaml
   791	        
   792	        attacher = UIAttacher(
   793	            layout=store.layout,
   794	            ui_roots={"comfyui": ui_root},
   795	        )
   796	        
   797	        yaml_path = ui_root / "extra_model_paths.yaml"
   798	        backup_path = ui_root / "extra_model_paths.yaml.synapse.bak"
   799	        
   800	        # Create original YAML with custom content
   801	        original_content = {"my_custom": {"base_path": "/my/custom/path", "loras": "loras"}}
   802	        with open(yaml_path, "w") as f:
   803	            yaml.dump(original_content, f)
   804	        original_bytes = yaml_path.read_bytes()
   805	        
   806	        # 1. First attach
   807	        result1 = attacher.attach("comfyui", use_yaml=True)
   808	        assert result1.success
   809	        assert backup_path.exists(), "Backup should be created on first attach"
   810	        backup_bytes_1 = backup_path.read_bytes()
   811	        
   812	        # Verify synapse section added
   813	        with open(yaml_path) as f:
   814	            content_after_attach1 = yaml.safe_load(f)
   815	        assert "synapse" in content_after_attach1
   816	        assert "my_custom" in content_after_attach1  # Original preserved
   817	        
   818	        # 2. Detach - should restore original
   819	        result2 = attacher.detach("comfyui")
   820	        assert result2.success
   821	        assert not backup_path.exists(), "Backup should be deleted after detach"
   822	        
   823	        # Verify original restored
   824	        restored_bytes = yaml_path.read_bytes()
   825	        assert restored_bytes == original_bytes, "Detach should restore exact original"
   826	        
   827	        # 3. Re-attach - should work and create new backup
   828	        result3 = attacher.attach("comfyui", use_yaml=True)
   829	        assert result3.success
   830	        assert backup_path.exists(), "Backup should be created on re-attach"
   831	        
   832	        # Backup should match the original
   833	        backup_bytes_2 = backup_path.read_bytes()
   834	        assert backup_bytes_2 == original_bytes, "Re-attach backup should be the original"
   835	        
   836	        # 4. Verify synapse section is back
   837	        with open(yaml_path) as f:
   838	            content_after_reattach = yaml.safe_load(f)
   839	        assert "synapse" in content_after_reattach
   840	        assert "my_custom" in content_after_reattach
   643	    """Test auto-restore from backup when using a pack."""
   644	
   645	    def test_use_restores_missing_blob_from_backup(self, tmp_path):
   646	        """When using a pack, missing blobs are auto-restored from backup."""
   647	        import hashlib
   648	
   649	        store = Store(tmp_path)
   650	        store.init()
   651	
   652	        # Create blob content and hash
   653	        content = b"model blob for auto-restore test"
   654	        sha256 = hashlib.sha256(content).hexdigest()
   655	
   656	        # Put blob ONLY on backup (simulates: user deleted local to save space)
   657	        backup_path = tmp_path / "backup"
   658	        blobs_dir = backup_path / ".synapse" / "store" / "data" / "blobs" / "sha256" / sha256[:2]
   659	        blobs_dir.mkdir(parents=True)
   660	        (blobs_dir / sha256).write_bytes(content)
   661	
   662	        config = BackupConfig(enabled=True, path=str(backup_path))
   663	        store.configure_backup(config)
   664	
   665	        # Create pack that references this blob
   666	        _create_pack_with_blob(store, "AutoRestorePack", sha256, len(content))
   667	
   668	        # Add pack to global profile
   669	        store.profile_service.add_pack_to_global("AutoRestorePack")
   670	
   671	        # Verify blob is NOT local yet
   672	        assert not store.blob_store.blob_exists(sha256)
   673	        assert store.blob_exists_on_backup(sha256)
   674	
   675	        # Use the pack - this should auto-restore
   676	        result = store.use("AutoRestorePack", sync=True)
   677	
   678	        # Verify blob is now local
   679	        assert store.blob_store.blob_exists(sha256)
   680	        assert "restored" in str(result.notes)
   681	
   682	    def test_use_falls_back_to_download_if_not_on_backup(self, tmp_path):
   683	        """If blob not on backup, use falls back to download (no crash)."""
   684	        import hashlib
   685	
   686	        store = Store(tmp_path)
   687	        store.init()
   688	
   689	        content = b"missing blob content"
   690	        sha256 = hashlib.sha256(content).hexdigest()
   691	
   692	        # Setup backup without the blob
   693	        backup_path = tmp_path / "backup"
   694	        backup_path.mkdir()
   695	        config = BackupConfig(enabled=True, path=str(backup_path))
   696	        store.configure_backup(config)
   697	
   698	        # Create pack referencing non-existent blob (no download URLs either)
   699	        _create_pack_with_blob(store, "MissingBlobPack", sha256, len(content))
   700	        store.profile_service.add_pack_to_global("MissingBlobPack")
   701	
   702	        # Use should not crash even if blob is nowhere
   703	        result = store.use("MissingBlobPack", sync=True)
   704	
   705	        # Blob still doesn't exist (no source to get it from)
   706	        assert not store.blob_store.blob_exists(sha256)
   707	
   708	    def test_use_without_backup_service_works(self, tmp_path):
   709	        """Use works when backup is disabled."""
   710	        store = Store(tmp_path)
   711	        store.init()
   712	
   713	        # Create pack with local blob
   714	        content = b"local blob for no-backup test"
   715	        sha256 = store.blob_store.adopt(_create_temp_file(tmp_path, content))
   716	
   717	        _create_pack_with_blob(store, "LocalPack", sha256, len(content))
   718	        store.profile_service.add_pack_to_global("LocalPack")
   719	
   720	        # Backup NOT configured
   721	        assert store.get_backup_status().enabled is False
   722	
   723	        # Use should work
   724	        result = store.use("LocalPack", sync=True)
   725	
   726	        assert result.synced is True

  2541	    resolved_filename = None

  2558	            download_url = f"https://civitai.com/api/download/models/{expected_vid}"
  2559	            logger.warning(
  2560	                "[download-asset] Lock URL stale (version_id mismatch), "
  2561	                "reconstructed: %s → %s", stale_url, download_url,
  2562	            )
  2563	
  2564	    # Last resort: construct URL from dependency's Civitai selector

  2581	
  2582	    logger.info(f"[download-asset] Downloading {filename} from {download_url[:100]}...")
  2583	    if store.blob_store.api_key:
  2584	        logger.debug(f"[download-asset] Using Civitai API key: {store.blob_store.api_key[:8]}...")
  2585	    
  2586	    # Create download tracking entry
  2587	    download_id = str(uuid.uuid4())[:8]
  2588	    _active_downloads[download_id] = {
  2589	        "download_id": download_id,
  2590	        "pack_name": pack_name,
  2591	        "asset_name": request.asset_name,
  2592	        "filename": filename,
  2593	        "status": "pending",
  2594	        "progress": 0.0,
  2595	        "downloaded_bytes": 0,
  2596	        "total_bytes": 0,
  2597	        "speed_bps": 0,
  2598	        "speed_mbps": 0,

  2602	        "completed_at": None,
  2603	        "target_path": None,

  2605	        "group_label": request.group_label,

  2608	    def do_download():

import { create } from 'zustand'

export interface PendingDownloadEntry {
  dependency_id: string
  sha256: string
  download_url: string
  size_bytes?: number | null
}

export interface UpdatePlanEntry {
  pack: string
  already_up_to_date: boolean
  changes: Array<{
    dependency_id: string
    old: Record<string, unknown>
    new: Record<string, unknown>
  }>
  ambiguous: Array<{
    dependency_id: string
    candidates: Array<{
      provider: string
      provider_model_id?: number
      provider_version_id?: number
      provider_file_id?: number
      sha256?: string
    }>
  }>
  pending_downloads: PendingDownloadEntry[]
  impacted_packs: string[]
}

export interface UpdateOptions {
  merge_previews: boolean
  update_description: boolean
  update_model_info: boolean
}

// --- Dismissed persistence ---
const DISMISSED_KEY = 'synapse-updates-dismissed'

function loadDismissed(): Record<string, string> {
  try {
    const raw = localStorage.getItem(DISMISSED_KEY)
    return raw ? JSON.parse(raw) : {}
  } catch {
    return {}
  }
}

function saveDismissed(dismissed: Record<string, string>) {
  try {
    localStorage.setItem(DISMISSED_KEY, JSON.stringify(dismissed))
  } catch {
    // Ignore localStorage errors
  }
}

/** Build a version key from plan changes for dismissed tracking */
function versionKeyFromPlan(plan: UpdatePlanEntry): string {
  const ids = [
    ...plan.changes.map(c => `${c.dependency_id}:${(c.new as Record<string, unknown>)?.provider_version_id ?? '?'}`),
    ...plan.ambiguous.map(a => `${a.dependency_id}:ambiguous`),
  ]
  return ids.sort().join(',')
}

interface CheckProgress {
  current: number
  total: number
  currentPack: string
}

interface UpdatesState {
  // Check state
  isChecking: boolean
  lastChecked: number | null
  checkError: string | null
  checkProgress: CheckProgress | null

  // Results - only packs with actual updates
  availableUpdates: Record<string, UpdatePlanEntry>

  // Selection for bulk operations (arrays for reliable React re-renders)
  selectedPacks: string[]

  // Apply state
  applyingPacks: string[]

  // Download group tracking
  activeGroupId: string | null

  // Dismissed updates persistence
  dismissedVersions: Record<string, string>

  // Computed
  updatesCount: number

  // Actions
  checkAll: () => Promise<void>
  selectPack: (name: string) => void
  deselectPack: (name: string) => void
  selectAll: () => void
  deselectAll: () => void
  togglePack: (name: string) => void
  applyUpdate: (packName: string, options?: Partial<UpdateOptions>) => Promise<boolean>
  applySelected: (options?: Partial<UpdateOptions>) => Promise<{ applied: number; failed: number }>
  retryDownloads: (packName: string) => Promise<boolean>
  retryAllPending: () => Promise<{ queued: number; failed: number }>
  dismissUpdate: (packName: string) => void
  cancelBatch: () => Promise<void>
  clearAll: () => void
}

/**
 * Queue downloads for changed dependencies via the existing download-asset endpoint.
 * This gives us progress tracking, Downloads tab integration, proper symlinks, etc.
 */
async function queueDownloadsForPack(
  packName: string,
  plan: UpdatePlanEntry,
  groupId?: string,
): Promise<void> {
  const changedDeps = [
    ...plan.changes.map(c => c.dependency_id),
    ...plan.ambiguous.map(a => a.dependency_id),
  ]

  for (const depId of changedDeps) {
    try {
      await fetch(`/api/packs/${encodeURIComponent(packName)}/download-asset`, {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify({
          asset_name: depId,
          ...(groupId ? { group_id: groupId, group_label: 'Pack Updates' } : {}),
        }),
      })
    } catch {
      // Download queue failure is non-fatal - user can retry from Downloads tab
    }
  }
}

/** Run async tasks with limited concurrency */
async function asyncPool<T>(
  concurrency: number,
  items: T[],
  fn: (item: T) => Promise<void>,
): Promise<void> {
  const executing = new Set<Promise<void>>()
  for (const item of items) {
    const p = fn(item).then(() => { executing.delete(p) })
    executing.add(p)
    if (executing.size >= concurrency) {
      await Promise.race(executing)
    }
  }
  await Promise.all(executing)
}

export const useUpdatesStore = create<UpdatesState>((set, get) => ({
  isChecking: false,
  lastChecked: null,
  checkError: null,
  checkProgress: null,
  availableUpdates: {},
  selectedPacks: [],
  applyingPacks: [],
  activeGroupId: null,
  dismissedVersions: loadDismissed(),
  updatesCount: 0,

  checkAll: async () => {
    set(() => ({ isChecking: true, checkError: null, checkProgress: null }))
    try {
      // Step 1: Fetch pack names
      const packsRes = await fetch('/api/packs/')
      if (!packsRes.ok) throw new Error('Failed to fetch packs')
      const packsData = await packsRes.json()
      const packNames: string[] = (packsData.packs || []).map((p: { name: string }) => p.name)

      if (packNames.length === 0) {
        set(() => ({
          isChecking: false,
          lastChecked: Date.now(),
          checkProgress: null,
          availableUpdates: {},
          updatesCount: 0,
          selectedPacks: [],
        }))
        return
      }

      // Step 2: Check each pack with concurrency of 3
      set(() => ({ checkProgress: { current: 0, total: packNames.length, currentPack: '' } }))

      const plans: Record<string, UpdatePlanEntry> = {}
      let current = 0

      await asyncPool(3, packNames, async (packName: string) => {
        set(() => ({ checkProgress: { current, total: packNames.length, currentPack: packName } }))
        try {
          const res = await fetch(`/api/updates/check/${encodeURIComponent(packName)}`)
          if (res.ok) {
            const data = await res.json()
            if (data.has_updates && data.plan) {
              plans[packName] = { pack: packName, ...data.plan }
            }
          }
        } catch {
          // Skip failed individual pack checks
        }
        current++
        set(() => ({ checkProgress: { current, total: packNames.length, currentPack: '' } }))
      })

      // Step 3: Filter dismissed updates
      const dismissed = { ...get().dismissedVersions }
      const allCheckedPacks = new Set(Object.keys(plans))
      let dismissedChanged = false
      for (const name of Object.keys(dismissed)) {
        if (!allCheckedPacks.has(name)) {
          delete dismissed[name]
          dismissedChanged = true
        }
      }
      if (dismissedChanged) {
        saveDismissed(dismissed)
      }

      const filtered: Record<string, UpdatePlanEntry> = {}
      for (const [name, plan] of Object.entries(plans)) {
        const hasPending = (plan.pending_downloads?.length ?? 0) > 0
        if (hasPending) {
          filtered[name] = plan
        } else {
          const key = versionKeyFromPlan(plan)
          if (!dismissed[name] || dismissed[name] !== key) {
            filtered[name] = plan
          }
        }
      }

      const filteredNames = Object.keys(filtered)

      set(() => ({
        isChecking: false,
        lastChecked: Date.now(),
        checkProgress: null,
        availableUpdates: filtered,
        updatesCount: filteredNames.length,
        selectedPacks: [...filteredNames],
        dismissedVersions: dismissed,
      }))
    } catch (e) {
      set(() => ({
        isChecking: false,
        checkProgress: null,
        checkError: e instanceof Error ? e.message : 'Unknown error',
      }))

   114	/**
   115	 * Queue downloads for changed dependencies via the existing download-asset endpoint.
   116	 * This gives us progress tracking, Downloads tab integration, proper symlinks, etc.
   117	 */
   118	async function queueDownloadsForPack(
   119	  packName: string,
   120	  plan: UpdatePlanEntry,
   121	  groupId?: string,
   122	): Promise<void> {
   123	  const changedDeps = [
   124	    ...plan.changes.map(c => c.dependency_id),
   125	    ...plan.ambiguous.map(a => a.dependency_id),
   126	  ]
   127	
   128	  for (const depId of changedDeps) {
   129	    try {
   130	      await fetch(`/api/packs/${encodeURIComponent(packName)}/download-asset`, {
   131	        method: 'POST',
   132	        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   133	        body: JSON.stringify({
   134	          asset_name: depId,
   135	          ...(groupId ? { group_id: groupId, group_label: 'Pack Updates' } : {}),
   136	        }),
   137	      })
   138	    } catch {
   139	      // Download queue failure is non-fatal - user can retry from Downloads tab
   140	    }
   141	  }
   142	}
   143	
   144	/** Run async tasks with limited concurrency */
   145	async function asyncPool<T>(
   288	  applyUpdate: async (packName: string, options?: Partial<UpdateOptions>) => {
   289	    set((state) => ({
   290	      applyingPacks: [...state.applyingPacks, packName],
   291	    }))
   292	
   293	    try {
   294	      // Step 1: Apply lock changes only (no sync - downloads go through download-asset)
   295	      const body: Record<string, unknown> = {
   296	        pack: packName,
   297	        sync: false,
   298	      }
   299	      if (options) {
   300	        body.options = options
   301	      }
   302	
   303	      const res = await fetch('/api/updates/apply', {
   304	        method: 'POST',
   305	        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   306	        body: JSON.stringify(body),
   307	      })
   308	
   309	      if (!res.ok) {
   310	        const errData = await res.json().catch(() => ({ detail: 'Unknown error' }))
   311	        throw new Error(errData.detail || 'Failed to apply update')
   312	      }
   313	      const result = await res.json()
   314	
   315	      if (result.applied) {
   316	        // Step 2: Queue downloads via existing download-asset endpoint
   317	        const groupId = `update-${Date.now()}`
   318	        const plan = get().availableUpdates[packName]
   319	        if (plan) {
   320	          await queueDownloadsForPack(packName, plan, groupId)
   321	        }
   322	
   323	        set((state) => {
   324	          const updates = { ...state.availableUpdates }
   325	          delete updates[packName]
   349	  applySelected: async (options?: Partial<UpdateOptions>) => {
   350	    const { selectedPacks, availableUpdates } = get()
   351	    const packs = [...selectedPacks]
   352	
   353	    if (packs.length === 0) return { applied: 0, failed: 0 }
   354	
   355	    // Generate group ID for all downloads in this batch
   356	    const groupId = `update-${Date.now()}`
   357	
   358	    // Mark all as applying
   359	    set((state) => ({
   360	      applyingPacks: [...new Set([...state.applyingPacks, ...packs])],
   361	    }))
   362	
   363	    try {
   364	      // Step 1: Apply all lock changes (no sync)
   365	      const body: Record<string, unknown> = {
   366	        packs,
   367	        sync: false,
   368	      }
   369	      if (options) {
   370	        body.options = options
   371	      }
   372	
   373	      const res = await fetch('/api/updates/apply-batch', {
   374	        method: 'POST',
   375	        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   376	        body: JSON.stringify(body),
   377	      })
   378	
   379	      if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Failed to apply batch update')
   380	      const result = await res.json()
   381	
   382	      // Step 2: Queue downloads for each successfully applied pack
   383	      const appliedPacks: string[] = []
   384	      for (const [packName, packResult] of Object.entries(result.results || {})) {
   385	        if ((packResult as Record<string, unknown>).applied) {
   386	          appliedPacks.push(packName)
   387	          const plan = availableUpdates[packName]
   388	          if (plan) {
   389	            await queueDownloadsForPack(packName, plan, groupId)
   390	          }
   391	        }
   392	      }
   452	      // Queue downloads for each pending dep via download-asset
   453	      // Pass the rebuilt URL from plan to override stale lock URLs
   454	      for (const pending of plan.pending_downloads) {
   455	        try {
   456	          await fetch(`/api/packs/${encodeURIComponent(packName)}/download-asset`, {
   457	            method: 'POST',
   458	            headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   459	            body: JSON.stringify({
   460	              asset_name: pending.dependency_id,
   461	              ...(pending.download_url ? { url: pending.download_url } : {}),
   462	              group_id: groupId,
   463	              group_label: 'Retry Downloads',
   464	            }),
   465	          })
   466	        } catch {
   467	          // Non-fatal per-dep failure
   468	        }
   469	      }
   470	
   471	      // Also queue downloads for regular changes if any
   472	      if (plan.changes.length > 0) {
   473	        await queueDownloadsForPack(packName, plan, groupId)
   474	      }

  4602	            # Create symlink with pack prefix to avoid conflicts
